book_claims.claim: Gluten-rich foods can trigger autoimmune reactions in susceptible individuals
book_claims.mechanism: Gliadin protein in gluten triggers inflammatory immune response in celiac patients

supplementary_research.finding: Celiac disease affects ~1% of the global population; gluten triggers intestinal permeability and systemic inflammation via gliadin-mediated zonulin release.
Non-celiac gluten sensitivity in ~6% of population shows gut dysbiosis and low-grade inflammation; sourdough preparations reduce gluten immunogenicity.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Poorly digested in celiac/NCGS; sourdough fermentation partially hydrolyzes gliadin
Fermentable fiber in whole-grain gluten foods feeds beneficial gut bacteria
Gliadin upregulates zonulin, increasing intestinal permeability in susceptible individuals

synergies.description: Combining gluten grains with legumes creates a complete amino acid profile while legume fiber moderates postprandial glucose
Polyphenols in olive oil reduce intestinal inflammation that may be triggered by gluten
Sourdough fermentation significantly reduces gliadin content and improves tolerance in NCGS
